rule_schema.standard_reference: Exact section or rule reference in standards/workspace.md.
rule_schema.section: Human section name from the standard.
rule_schema.title: Short operational title.
rule_schema.requirement: Normative requirement extracted from the standard.
rule_schema.applies_to: Repo surfaces where the rule applies.
rule_schema.trigger_kinds: Change categories that should pull this rule into an applicable checklist.
rule_schema.phases: Workflow phases where this rule is relevant.
rule_schema.enforcement_mode: validator-required, checklist-required, checklist-and-validator, advisory, or backlog.
rule_schema.evidence_required: What an implementation or verification receipt should record when applicable.
rule_schema.automation: Current or intended mechanical enforcement owner.

rules.title: Keep app-dev as one control workspace
rules.requirement: app-dev is a single control workspace where governance, standards, templates, scripts, and all application projects live in one repository with one git history, one .codex governance layer, one branch-protection policy, and one CI pipeline.
evidence_required: Changed workspace surfaces remain in the root repository.
No additional governance layer, repository history, or CI pipeline is introduced without an explicit recorded exception.

rules.title: Track apps directly under projects
rules.requirement: projects/<app>/ directories are tracked directly in app-dev and are never initialized as nested or standalone git repositories.
evidence_required: New or changed apps are under projects/<app>/ in this repository.
No nested .git directory or standalone repository metadata was introduced.

rules.title: Scope app governance files to the app
rules.requirement: Each app keeps its own AGENTS.md, specs/, and package.json scoped to its directory. Each implementation spec owns its own plan.md inside specs/NNN-<slug>/, and gated checklists live inside the active spec folder.
evidence_required: App AGENTS.md, package.json, and specs are scoped to the app directory.
Spec plan and checklist files live inside the active numbered spec folder.

rules.title: Keep app ignores local to each app
rules.requirement: Each app's .gitignore excludes its own node_modules/, build output, and test artifacts.
evidence_required: App-local generated dependency, build, and test artifact paths are ignored by the app.

rules.title: Add shared app code only after real reuse exists
rules.requirement: Shared code across apps goes under packages/ in this same repo only after at least two apps share real code such as ui, auth, api-client, or config.
evidence_required: Any packages/ introduction is justified by at least two apps sharing real code.
The active plan records why shared code belongs in packages/.

rules.title: Promote to pnpm or Turborepo only after threshold
rules.requirement: Promote to a pnpm/Turborepo workspace only after at least two apps share real code, or record an explicit repository decision.
evidence_required: Any pnpm or Turborepo adoption records the sharing threshold or explicit exception decision.

rules.title: Record repository split exceptions
rules.requirement: An app may split into its own repository only for a hard external requirement, and the same change must update standards/workspace.md and the active spec plan.md.
evidence_required: The hard external requirement for a repo split is recorded.
standards/workspace.md and the active spec plan.md are updated in the same change.

rules.title: Keep root layout aligned with the authoritative tree
rules.requirement: Anything present in the repo but absent from the authoritative layout is either app-internal or a drift candidate; anything in the layout but absent from the repo is a defect.
evidence_required: Changed or new root paths are represented in the authoritative layout or justified as app-internal.
Required layout paths exist.

rules.title: Treat the workspace manifest as machine-readable layout
rules.requirement: The workspace manifest is the machine-readable form of the authoritative layout.
evidence_required: Required root paths and governed numbers are updated in standards/workspace-manifest.psd1 when the layout changes.
Validators consume the manifest rather than a duplicate path list where applicable.

rules.title: Keep one owner for each governed fact
rules.requirement: Every governed fact has exactly one owning file. Other files may point to the owner; they may not restate its content.
evidence_required: Changed documentation points to the owning file instead of duplicating governed facts.
Any duplicated owned fact is removed or justified by a validator-backed contract.

rules.title: Point workflow facts to the workflow standard
rules.requirement: Workflow phases, Lean and Gated paths, convergence, and planning rules are owned by standards/spec-driven-workflow.md.
evidence_required: Workflow semantics changes are made in standards/spec-driven-workflow.md.
AGENTS.md, SKILL.md, and README retain pointers rather than copied procedures.

rules.title: Keep command steps in .agents commands
rules.requirement: Executable workflow steps per phase are owned by .agents/commands/<phase>.md.
evidence_required: Executable phase steps are updated in the matching .agents/commands/<phase>.md file.
Standards and skills point to command files instead of copying command procedures.

rules.title: Keep default stack in the stack standard
rules.requirement: The default stack is owned by standards/stack.md.
evidence_required: Stack changes are made in standards/stack.md.
Other surfaces point to the stack owner or summarize without conflicting.

rules.title: Keep module contract in the skill reference
rules.requirement: The module contract is owned by the local skill reference references/module-contract.md.
evidence_required: Module contract edits are made in the skill reference owner.
AGENTS.md and template AGENTS.md files do not become competing owners.

rules.title: Keep scripting rules in the scripting standard
rules.requirement: Scripting and coding rules are owned by standards/scripting.md.
evidence_required: Script mechanics changes are made in standards/scripting.md or its registry.
Other documents point to the scripting standard instead of duplicating the rule body.

rules.title: Keep command policy in hooks and rules
rules.requirement: Command-execution policy is owned by .codex/hooks/pre-command.ps1 plus .codex/rules/default.rules, with layering described in section 6.
evidence_required: Command policy changes are made in the hook rule table and default rules where applicable.
README and AGENTS.md do not become alternate policy owners.

rules.title: Keep AGENTS size limit synchronized
rules.requirement: The AGENTS.md size limit is one number in the manifest; .codex/config.toml project_doc_max_bytes and the validator must agree. The adopted value is 32768.
evidence_required: AgentsMaxBytes, project_doc_max_bytes, and validator expectations agree.
Any change to the adopted value updates all owning and consuming surfaces.

rules.title: Keep root AGENTS as durable rules and pointers
rules.requirement: Root AGENTS.md contains durable workspace rules and pointers, not detailed procedures or duplicated workflow steps.
evidence_required: Root AGENTS.md changes remain pointer-oriented for workflow detail.
Detailed procedures remain in standards or command files.

rules.title: Keep app AGENTS scoped to app identity
rules.requirement: projects/<app>/AGENTS.md contains app identity, active-spec pointer, app-specific constraints, and verification baseline, with repo-relative forward-slash paths.
evidence_required: App AGENTS.md files use repo-relative forward-slash paths.
App AGENTS.md files do not duplicate root governance procedure text.

rules.title: Keep wrapper skills thin
rules.requirement: Wrapper skills are classifiable and receipt-referenced; they delegate to canonical standards and never accumulate their own procedure text.
evidence_required: Wrapper skill changes preserve delegation to canonical standards.
Receipt references identify the relevant wrapper without copying procedure text into it.

rules.title: Keep executable workflow in commands
rules.requirement: .agents/commands/ is the executable workflow surface. Each command states working directory, exact script invocations, receipt obligations, and closure rule.
evidence_required: Command changes preserve working directory, script invocation, receipt, and closure-rule contracts.
Workflow execution details are not moved into AGENTS.md or wrapper skills.

rules.title: Keep .codex hook files wired
rules.requirement: .codex/config.toml uses inline hooks only; hooks.json must not coexist. Every hook file that exists is wired to an event in config.toml; unwired hook files are deleted.
evidence_required: .codex/config.toml contains the active hook registrations.
No .codex/hooks.json is introduced while inline hooks are used.
Every committed hook file is registered or removed.

rules.title: Pair standards with enforcement or label as preference
rules.requirement: standards/ contains canonical prose and scripts/ contains mechanical enforcement. A standard without an enforcing script is a preference; label it as such or build the script.
evidence_required: New or changed standard requirements name their enforcement owner or are clearly labeled as preference.
Validator changes ship with the standard changes they enforce.

rules.title: Keep path and content rules in the manifest
rules.requirement: Required paths, content rules, CI-content rules, and governed numbers live in standards/workspace-manifest.psd1 and are consumed by check-workspace.ps1 and validate-codex-assets.ps1.
evidence_required: Required path, content, CI-content, and governed-number changes are represented in standards/workspace-manifest.psd1.
check-workspace.ps1 and validate-codex-assets.ps1 consume the manifest for applicable data.

rules.title: Validate audit records as path-exists only
rules.requirement: Historical records under docs/audit/ are validated as path-exists only.
evidence_required: Validators do not enforce mutable content rules against immutable audit records.
Audit record validation remains path-exists only.

rules.title: Use check-all as the governance entry point
rules.requirement: scripts/check-all.ps1 is the single entry point that runs the full governance sequence in CI order.
evidence_required: Humans, README, AGENTS.md, and CI invoke scripts/check-all.ps1 rather than maintaining separate governance command lists.
check-all order remains aligned with CI order.

rules.title: Enforce root governance source immediately
rules.requirement: Root governance validators enforce current app-dev source surfaces immediately: standards, commands, templates, skills, hooks, CI, and root scripts.
evidence_required: Root governance source changes pass current source validators.
Compatibility exceptions are not applied to root governance source surfaces.

rules.title: Keep project artifact validators scoped to projects
rules.requirement: Project artifact validators operate against app state under projects/<app>/.
evidence_required: Project validators accept a project path and inspect app state under projects/<app>/.
Root source validators are not conflated with project artifact checks.

rules.title: Do not retroactively break existing apps
rules.requirement: Existing apps are not made retroactively noncompliant because the root workspace later tightens template density or command wording. Their default contract is validator compatibility mode.
evidence_required: Validator changes preserve compatibility mode for established apps.
Any stricter app contract is opt-in or limited to new/generated/refreshed apps.

rules.title: Use current-template mode for new or refreshed apps
rules.requirement: Newly generated apps, disposable scaffold verification projects, and explicitly refreshed apps are validated against the current template contract.
evidence_required: New, disposable, or explicitly refreshed apps are validated against the current template contract.
Compatibility mode is not used to mask defects in newly generated artifacts.

rules.title: Execute CI workflows only from root .github workflows
rules.requirement: CI workflows execute only from .github/workflows/ at the repository root. projects/<app>/.github/workflows/ must not exist.
evidence_required: CI workflow files exist only under root .github/workflows/.
No project-local executable workflow directory is introduced.

rules.title: Use one validation workflow with workspace and app jobs
rules.requirement: The validation workflow has workspace-validation and app-validation job families, with app validation running from matrix project working directories.
evidence_required: Validation workflow keeps distinct workspace and app validation job families.
App jobs run with working-directory set from the project matrix path.

rules.title: Add app matrix discovery only after a second app
rules.requirement: Matrix entries are hand-listed while one app exists; when the second app lands under projects/, replace the list with a discovery job that emits the matrix from projects/*/package.json.
evidence_required: While only one app exists, the CI matrix remains hand-listed.
When a second app is added, the CI matrix is replaced by project package.json discovery.

rules.title: Keep templates free of executable workflows
rules.requirement: Templates do not ship executable workflow files. CI reference snippets live in templates/common/ci/ with non-executable names, and create-app.ps1 excludes .github/ from template copies.
evidence_required: Template CI examples are non-executable reference snippets under templates/common/ci/.
create-app.ps1 does not copy template .github/ workflow files into generated apps.

rules.title: Preserve CI workflow hygiene
rules.requirement: CI workflows use concurrency with cancel-in-progress per ref, timeout-minutes on every job, setup-node npm caching keyed to each app lockfile, Playwright browser caching, and failure artifact uploads for playwright-report/ and test-results/.
evidence_required: CI jobs include concurrency, timeout-minutes, dependency caching, Playwright caching, and failure artifact upload where applicable.

rules.title: Keep ubuntu-latest as the CI portability contract
rules.requirement: CI runs on ubuntu-latest; Linux execution is the portability contract.
evidence_required: CI remains on ubuntu-latest for required validation.
Windows CI, if added, is additive and does not replace Linux validation.

rules.title: Run app e2e without backend secrets by default
rules.requirement: App e2e in CI runs in the unconfigured-env state by default: no backend secrets in CI, and e2e specs pass against degraded and empty states.
evidence_required: CI e2e paths do not require backend secrets by default.
Changed e2e coverage passes against degraded and empty app states where applicable.

rules.title: Prove lockfiles with npm ci on Linux
rules.requirement: Lockfiles are proven by npm ci on Linux in CI; a lockfile that installs locally but not in CI is defective.
evidence_required: Changed lockfiles are validated by npm ci on Linux CI or equivalent local reproduction when CI is unavailable.

rules.title: Maintain hook and execpolicy layers
rules.requirement: Two enforcement layers exist by design: the pre-command hook evaluates matched Codex tool calls, and .codex/rules/default.rules governs commands escalated outside the sandbox.
evidence_required: Command policy changes preserve both Codex hook and escalation-rule coverage where applicable.
The hook remains stricter where its sandbox context requires extra protection.

rules.title: Update command policy layers in one commit
rules.requirement: When adding a policy, add the hook rule, add the execpolicy rule if the command can be escalated, and add a blocked-case to test-hooks.ps1 in one commit.
evidence_required: New command policy includes hook coverage, escalation-rule coverage where applicable, and a blocked-case test.
Any accepted layer divergence is only in the hook-stricter direction and is documented.

rules.title: Fail closed on malformed hook input
rules.requirement: Hooks fail closed: malformed input produces a block decision, never a crash or silent allow.
evidence_required: Hook tests cover malformed input and assert a block decision.

rules.title: Use workflow standard and commands as planning pointers
rules.requirement: Planning and spec workflow definitions live in standards/spec-driven-workflow.md, execution lives in .agents/commands/, and enforcement runs through the named spec and receipt validators.
evidence_required: Workflow behavior changes update the owner standard or command file.
Relevant validators remain wired for spec artifacts, analysis, receipts, and app verification.

rules.title: Treat workflow receipts as required evidence
rules.requirement: workflow-receipts.md in the active spec folder is the required evidence trail; agent narratives are not evidence.
evidence_required: Active spec workflow-receipts.md records required evidence for the slice.
Final handoff does not substitute chat narrative for receipt evidence.

rules.title: Use hosted CI as final referee
rules.requirement: Hosted CI on the pushed head is the final referee; local verification does not substitute for a green run.
evidence_required: Final handoff distinguishes local verification from hosted CI state.
If hosted CI is unavailable or not yet green, the handoff reports that residual gap.

rules.title: Use repo-relative forward-slash paths in committed artifacts
rules.requirement: All committed docs, ledgers, configs, and generated artifacts use repo-relative forward-slash paths. Absolute local paths are defects, including inside comments.
evidence_required: Changed committed artifacts use repo-relative forward-slash paths.
No absolute local paths are introduced in docs, configs, comments, ledgers, or generated files.

rules.title: Use cross-platform README command examples
rules.requirement: README command examples use cross-platform pwsh forms such as ./scripts/check-all.ps1 and cd projects/my-app.
evidence_required: README command examples use cross-platform pwsh and repo-relative forms.

rules.title: Append corrections to audit records
rules.requirement: Audit records under docs/audit/ are immutable once their slice closes; corrections append, never rewrite.
evidence_required: Closed audit records are not rewritten for corrections.
Any correction is appended with clear context.

rules.title: Fix standard and reality drift in the same change
rules.requirement: When reality and a standard diverge, the same commit updates whichever one is wrong. When a normative standard has a matching registry file, reconcile the prose standard and registry in the same slice.
evidence_required: Detected standard/reality drift is resolved in the same change where practical.
Normative prose-standard changes reconcile the matching registry file in the same slice unless the change is purely editorial.
If deferred, the handoff records the exact residual drift and owner.

rules.title: Treat governance surface changes as governance changes
rules.requirement: Changes to .codex/, .agents/, root AGENTS.md, standards/, the manifest, templates, CI, or scripts/ are governance changes.
evidence_required: Governance changes run the canonical governance check sequence before commit.
Final handoff identifies governance verification results and any skipped checks.

rules.title: Separate governance slices from app feature slices
rules.requirement: Governance-layer changes stay in slices separate from app feature slices except where an app-discovered defect is fixed at the template or harness source in the same slice and recorded in the receipt.
evidence_required: Governance and app feature work are separated unless a paired template or harness fix is explicitly recorded.
Receipts record any allowed pairing between app work and governance source fixes.
